req.title: System-Admin views leave-request
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a leave-request through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that a system-admin can view a leave-request via UI, mandatory fields are validated, and an audit record is written on success. I designed five tests covering: (1) happy-path admin view with audit verification (EP/positive), (2) unauthenticated access returning 401 (NEG/negative), (3) forbidden role returning 403 (NEG/negative), (4) boundary on leave-request ID at minimum valid value (BVA/boundary), and (5) state-transition from 'pending' status visible in view form (STC/positive). Each test is self-contained using fixtures and helper placeholders, kept under 40 lines.

tests.name: test_admin_views_leave_request_success
preconditions: At least one leave-request exists in the system
actions: Login as system-admin via <admin_session_fixture>
Navigate to /leave-requests
Select an existing leave-request by ID
Assert the leave-request detail form is visible
Assert mandatory fields are populated
Assert audit record was written via <audit_api_helper>
expected: Leave-request detail page renders with HTTP 200
Mandatory fields (employee, dates, reason) are displayed
Audit record contains actor=admin and action=VIEW
tests.executable:
def test_admin_views_leave_request_success(admin_page, existing_leave_request, audit_api_helper):
    page = admin_page
    lr_id = existing_leave_request["id"]

    page.goto(f"/leave-requests/{lr_id}")
    assert page.url.endswith(f"/leave-requests/{lr_id}")

    assert page.locator("[data-testid='field-employee']").is_visible()
    assert page.locator("[data-testid='field-start-date']").is_visible()
    assert page.locator("[data-testid='field-end-date']").is_visible()
    assert page.locator("[data-testid='field-reason']").is_visible()

    displayed_id = page.locator("[data-testid='entity-id']").inner_text()
    assert displayed_id == str(lr_id)

    audit = audit_api_helper.get_latest_audit(entity_id=lr_id)
    assert audit["action"] == "VIEW"
    assert audit["actor_role"] == "admin"
    assert audit["status_code"] in (200, 201)

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_leave_request
preconditions: No active session / user is not authenticated
actions: Ensure browser has no auth cookies via <clear_session_fixture>
Attempt to navigate directly to /leave-requests/<id>
Assert redirect to login page or 401 response
expected: User is redirected to /login or receives HTTP 401
Leave-request detail form is NOT rendered
No audit record written for VIEW action
tests.executable:
def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_leave_request(browser_page, existing_leave_request, audit_api_helper):
    page = browser_page
    clear_session(page)
    lr_id = existing_leave_request["id"]

    response = page.goto(f"/leave-requests/{lr_id}")

    is_redirected_to_login = "/login" in page.url
    is_401 = response is not None and response.status == 401
    assert is_redirected_to_login or is_401, (
        f"Expected redirect to /login or 401, got url={page.url} status={getattr(response, 'status', 'N/A')}"
    )

    assert not page.locator("[data-testid='field-employee']").is_visible()

    audit = audit_api_helper.get_latest_audit(entity_id=lr_id, action="VIEW")
    assert audit is None, "No VIEW audit record should exist for unauthenticated access"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_leave_request
preconditions: User is authenticated with role=employee (not admin)
A valid leave-request ID exists that belongs to a different employee
actions: Login as user with role=employee via <employee_session_fixture>
Navigate to /leave-requests/<id> of another employee's request
Assert access is denied with 403 or forbidden page
expected: HTTP 403 response or forbidden UI message is shown
Leave-request detail form is NOT rendered
No VIEW audit record is written
tests.executable:
def test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_leave_request(employee_page, other_employee_leave_request, audit_api_helper):
    page = employee_page
    lr_id = other_employee_leave_request["id"]

    response = page.goto(f"/leave-requests/{lr_id}")

    is_forbidden_page = page.locator("[data-testid='error-403']").is_visible()
    is_403 = response is not None and response.status == 403
    assert is_forbidden_page or is_403, (
        f"Expected 403 or forbidden UI, got url={page.url} status={getattr(response, 'status', 'N/A')}"
    )

    assert not page.locator("[data-testid='field-employee']").is_visible()

    audit = audit_api_helper.get_latest_audit(entity_id=lr_id, action="VIEW")
    assert audit is None, "No VIEW audit record should exist for forbidden role access"

tests.name: test_admin_views_leave_request_with_minimum_valid_id
preconditions: A leave-request with the minimum valid ID (ID=1) exists
actions: Login as system-admin via <admin_session_fixture>
Navigate to /leave-requests/1 (minimum valid entity ID)
Assert the detail form loads correctly
Assert audit record is written for entity ID=1
expected: Leave-request detail page renders with HTTP 200
Audit record written with entity_id=1 and action=VIEW
trace: BVA: minimum boundary of entity ID domain
tests.executable:
def test_admin_views_leave_request_with_minimum_valid_id(admin_page, ensure_leave_request_id_1, audit_api_helper):
    page = admin_page
    min_id = 1

    response = page.goto(f"/leave-requests/{min_id}")
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected 200, got {response.status}"

    displayed_id = page.locator("[data-testid='entity-id']").inner_text()
    assert displayed_id == str(min_id), f"Expected entity id={min_id}, got {displayed_id}"

    assert page.locator("[data-testid='field-employee']").is_visible()
    assert page.locator("[data-testid='field-start-date']").is_visible()
    assert page.locator("[data-testid='field-end-date']").is_visible()

    audit = audit_api_helper.get_latest_audit(entity_id=min_id, action="VIEW")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must exist for minimum ID view"
    assert audit["entity_id"] == min_id
    assert audit["actor_role"] == "admin"

tests.name: test_admin_views_leave_request_in_pending_state
preconditions: A leave-request exists in 'pending' status (initial state)
actions: Login as system-admin via <admin_session_fixture>
Navigate to /leave-requests/<pending_lr_id>
Assert the status field displays 'pending'
Assert all mandatory fields are visible
Assert audit record is written with status=pending snapshot
expected: Mandatory fields visible: employee, start-date, end-date, reason, status
Audit record written with action=VIEW and entity state=pending
trace: R-WEB-016: mandatory fields must be validated
STC: covers the initial 'pending' state of the leave-request lifecycle
tests.executable:
def test_admin_views_leave_request_in_pending_state(admin_page, pending_leave_request, audit_api_helper):
    page = admin_page
    lr_id = pending_leave_request["id"]

    page.goto(f"/leave-requests/{lr_id}")

    status_field = page.locator("[data-testid='field-status']")
    assert status_field.is_visible()
    assert status_field.inner_text().strip().lower() == "pending"

    for field in ["field-employee", "field-start-date", "field-end-date", "field-reason"]:
        assert page.locator(f"[data-testid='{field}']").is_visible(), f"{field} must be visible"

    audit = audit_api_helper.get_latest_audit(entity_id=lr_id, action="VIEW")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written on successful view"
    assert audit["entity_state"] == "pending"
    assert audit["actor_role"] == "admin"
